channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: Four Laos villagers freed from cave, two remain missing
item.description: The four villagers crawled out of the cave just after 3pm local time, after water levels were able to be drained from the cave's flooded sections.

media:group.media:description: The villagers were found by cave divers after being trapped in a cave in Xaisomboun province, in central Laos.

item.title: Remains found in search for missing man Peter Willoughby
item.description: Tasmania Police has confirmed a member of a volunteer search group has found what are believed to be human remains in the search for a missing man in Tasmania's north.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Smith and Scout Wallen

media:group.media:description: Peter Willoughby, 76, went missing in October last year.

item.title: Angus Taylor draws 'battle lines' in key Liberal Party address
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor accuses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese of starting a war on aspiration with the recent federal budget.

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor has declared his party is the only one capable of replacing the Labor government.

item.title: Tasmanian minister Madeleine Ogilvie to move to the backbench
item.description: Tasmanian government minister Madeleine Ogilvie will move to the backbench after accusations she misled parliament.
item.dc:creator: Lucy MacDonald

media:group.media:description: Madeleine Ogilvie has been accused of misleading the Tasmanian parliament.

item.title: Donald Trump just passed his latest medical check. Here's what's in the report
item.description: Donald Trump's medical report shows the US president underwent a CT scan, cancer screenings and other preventative assessments carried out by 22 specialists.
item.dc:creator: Lewis Wiseman

media:group.media:description: The medical report says the US president is in excellent health.

item.title: Elderly woman dies in Gold Coast resort fire
item.description: Five other people were also treated at the scene after smoke carried through three storeys of the high-rise building.
item.dc:creator: Heloise Vyas

media:group.media:description: About 20 firefighters responded to a unit fire in a Broadbeach resort as dozens of tenants were evacuated.

item.title: Lil' Kim brings nostalgia to Vivid Sydney as festival continues
item.description: Rap superstar Lil' Kim performed mostly truncated versions of her hits at her Vivid Sydney show, but she gave the diehard fans in the audience exactly what they came for.
item.dc:creator: Mawunyo Gbogbo

media:group.media:description: It's been 15 years since Lil' Kim last performed in Australia.

item.title: Man arrested after house destroyed by fire
item.description: A man in his 20s has been arrested after a suspicious fire destroyed a house and damaged two others north of Brisbane.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: Ten crews worked to contain the blaze that destroyed a house and damaged two adjoining properties.

item.title: AFL concedes umpire error as Cats lament 'howler' call
item.description: Geelong's Ollie Dempsey was denied a clear goal in the narrow loss to Carlton when umpires chose not to call on the AFL Review Centre to check a decision.

media:group.media:description: Despite Ollie Dempsey clearly kicking a goal off his shin, a behind was given.

item.title: Pedestrian seriously injured as ute mounts footpath along coastal walkway
item.description: Onlookers describe their shock after a pedestrian was mowed down by a ute that mounted a popular coastal footpath in Perth's northern suburbs on Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Kate Christian and Andrew Hanlon

media:group.media:description: Multiple emergency vehicles were on the scene after a pedestrian was hit on West Coast Drive in North Beach.

item.title: Live: 'I'm about to leave': Bunker denies Bulldogs with controversial call
item.description: The NRL Bunker has controversially denied the Canterbury Bulldogs a try that could have levelled their match against Wests Tigers, with two-time premiership winner Luke Lewis describing the call as "rubbish". Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Josh Curran charges down a kick from Jarome Luai, Wests Tigers vs Canterbury Bulldogs, Saturday, May 30, 2026.

item.title: US won't let China impose 'hegemony' in Asia, Hegseth says
item.description: The US Defense Secretary addressed Asia's most important defence summit, but did not mention Taiwan as questions remain over a stalled weapons package.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Pete Hegseth spoke at the International Institute for Strategic Studies Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore on Saturday.

item.title: Will AI become conscious? What a philosopher and a neuroscientist think
item.description: The question "can computers think" is something many people are taking very seriously. A philosophy professor believes we could be heading towards a future with conscious machines, while a neuroscientist is sceptical.
item.dc:creator: Wren Gillett

media:group.media:description: In an experiment, many participants couldn't work out if they were having a conversation with a human or AI.

item.title: Officers, civilians 'fortunate' to avoid injury after police station crash
item.description: A court has heard Donny Hobbs, 67, told police he would drive through Mudgee Police Station, in western New South Wales, shortly before the alleged incident.
item.dc:creator: Catherine James and Courtney Yeandle

media:group.media:description: The ute crashed into Mudgee Police Station on Friday afternoon.

item.title: Hindley conquers 'epic' day to climb onto Giro podium
item.description: Australia's Jai Hindley moved to third overall in the 2026 Giro d'Italia after a "tough day out" on stage 19, helped by a superb sacrificial ride by teammate Giulio Pellizzari.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jai Hindley climbed into third place on the Giro d'Italia after an "epic" 19th stage.

item.title: Families of police officers killed at Wieambilla feel 'let down' as property sold
item.description: The families of two Queensland police officers shot dead by extremists in 2022 say they feel "deeply let down" after learning through a public real estate listing the property had been sold to a private buyer.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: Matthew Arnold and Rachel McCrow were shot dead while responding to a missing persons report in 2022.

item.title: Live: Freo storms to statement victory to send alarm bells ringing for Lions
item.description: The ladder-leading Dockers notch a 25-point victory over the Lions to condemn the premiers to a third consecutive defeat. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony and Andrew McGarry

media:group.media:description: The Dockers surged clear after half-time.

item.title: Pleas for French Open to move 'unsafe' ad boards after star's nasty fall
item.description: Iga Świątek urges French Open officials to move advertising boards away from the courts after seeing Zeynep Sönmez's tournament end due to injury.

media:group.media:description: Zeynep Sönmez's doubles campaign came to an end after she took a nasty fall on the advertising hoardings placed by the baseline.

item.title: Matthew Perry was 'on the right path' before inner circle betrayed him
item.description: A trusted assistant, two doctors, a middleman and the "Ketamine Queen": How Matthew Perry's inner circle preyed on the Friends star's addiction before his death.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: Beloved actor Matthew Perry died at age 54 in 2023.

item.title: The 1970s oil shock gave birth to permaculture. This fuel crisis is reviving it
item.description: The tenets of permaculture are attracting a new generation of home gardeners. It's perhaps no surprise, considering the movement has firm roots in the world's last oil crisis in the 1970s.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Loney

media:group.media:description: David Holmgren with a newly planted and stone mulched fruit tree at Jackeys Marsh, Tasmania in 1977.

item.title: Woman charged for trying to print child abuse material at Canberra store
item.description: A NSW woman is arrested in Canberra after staff at a photo printing business allegedly discovered child abuse material she tried and failed to print due to a machine malfunction.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: Police allege the 36-year-old left the photo printing store after being refunded for the machine's failure to print the child abuse material.

item.title: 'Ridiculous increases': Cattle market kicks in the rain
item.description: Cattle prices jumped significantly this week as rain fell across key production areas, especially in New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brann

media:group.media:description: Cattle prices are rising thanks to some good May rain.

item.title: Ukraine war reaches 'a new phase', where Kyiv has the advantage
item.description: Ukraine has developed AI-enabled drones capable of making precision strikes from hundreds of kilometres away, and it means using Russia's vital logistics routes has become a dangerous "lottery".
item.dc:creator: Riley Stuart

media:group.media:description: Another Russian military vehicle is stopped in its tracks near Donetsk.

item.title: Australia's marathon queen thought she might never walk again
item.description: As a girl, Kaz Thorburn was told she would never play sport or have children. The mother of three has now run 551 marathons.
item.dc:creator: James Tugwell

media:group.media:description: Kaz Thorburn began running as an experiment.

item.title: Vivid Sydney drone shows cancelled after technical failure
item.description: All remaining Vivid Sydney drone shows have been cancelled following a technical failure at Monday night's show, where almost 90 drones fell out of the sky.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Corvo

media:group.media:description: Almost 90 drones fell out of the sky at Monday night's Vivid Sydney show.
